hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — Q, 4. You kicked off the action with a raise, and your opponent decided to call, setting the stage for a thrilling hand.
Flop — King, 7, 2. You bet to test the waters, and your opponent opted to call, keeping their intentions hidden.
Turn — 4. You bet again, trying to build the pot, but your opponent raised, signaling they were still very much in the game.
River — King. With the final community card in place, your opponent moved all-in, putting maximum pressure on you to make a tough decision.

wrongReasons: 10 8 has little reason to lead the river here — one pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
3 9 has little reason to lead the river here — one pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
5 9 has little reason to lead the river here — one pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: K 7

Your opponent flopped Two Pair and played it cautiously, calling on the flop to disguise their hand. On the river, they made a Full House with the King, and then moved all-in, turning their earlier restraint into an aggressive trap.
